弗兰克·雅各布是一个在疯狂杂志的57年里嘲笑流行的文化和政治，往往是在塔拉曼的4月5日去世的现场文化和政治，常常在Tarzana去世。他是91岁。
  Jacobs先生带来了一个快速的机智，一个深入的想法和对1957年押韵的热爱，成为一个聪明的幽默杂志最多的贡献者之一，特别是在20世纪60年代和70年代，当它在高峰期间它的不敬及其文化影响。
 “他是终极工匠，”曾疯狂的编辑器.John Ficarra说。 “他可能会肯定会忽视，但你必须尊重他：他是一个无尽的搜索完美的词，完美的短语和完美的押韵。”
 与艺术家这样的艺术家（去年去世），乔治·伍德布里奇和格里Gerry Gersten先生，Jacobs先生像“屋顶上的小提琴手一样”（他在屋顶上的“天线”中变成了郊区的送达）;批评罗纳德里根总统的政策在Poe的“乌鸦”的一行逐行讽刺;像透明的办公室工作人员迪尔伯特一样（他的小隔间缺乏通风窒息的漫画人物的易魅族人物（他的死亡）和醉酒阶层是醉酒的，写道的杂种并为功能失调的家庭设计了圣诞颂歌。
  一个前疯狂的作家Arnie Kogen在接受采访时说：“弗兰克的东西闪闪发光。它很聪明，优雅，总是有趣。他是有史以来最好的作家疯狂杂志。“
 音乐剧的粉丝，Jacobs先生与Drucker先生一起转变为“东侧故事”转变为“东侧故事”，在美国和苏联领导的帮派之间的联合国的音乐斗争。苏联总理（和帮派领袖）Nikita Khrushchev Sang：
 当你是一个红色的时候，你从你的第一个派对清洗到你的最后一个力量的红色！当你是一个红色的时候，你有Galore的代理商;在激发战争时，你会给奖品。 
Jacobs先生的伟大美国歌曲的模仿促使欧文柏林和一群歌曲出版商代表Cole Porter，Rodgers和Hammerstein等工作，以苏疯狂的母公司，例如，出版物，以获取版权侵权。
 问题是“疯狂地唱歌”，这是1961年发表的一个撤销部分，由Jacobs先生和拉里·塞格尔先生完全由歌曲明星组成。其中包括“Louella Schwartz描述了她的Malady”（柏林的一个讽刺的“一个漂亮的女孩就像一个旋律”）和“我第一次看到Maris”（杰罗姆·克恩和奥斯卡Hammerstein II的“我最后一次看到的巴黎“），关于Yankee Slugger Roger Maris的商业化在赛季中，他击中了一个录制的61家家庭跑步。
 他与Gillette签订了一份合同，以插上剃刀刀片。当他发现他砍了自己，他去了和插上了辅助工具！我最后一次看到Maris他插上了六个品牌的啤酒！民主党应该付钱给他插上新的边境。
 1964年，美国上诉法院的第二次巡回赛决定疯了。
  在他看来，在欧文R.考夫曼（最着名的朱利叶斯和罗森伯格的间谍试验）中判断欧文·卢尔福夫人写道，“被告人的明显被写入与原告的组成相同的仪表，如果原版是似乎是不可避免的认可，但这样的理由甚至是必要的;我们怀疑，即使是杰出的作曲家也应该被允许允许欧文柏林的作曲家申请在Iambic Pentameter的财产权益。“
  富兰克林·雅各布于1929年5月30日出生，在林肯，奈布尔·纳布尔。他的父亲大卫是一家旅游推销员，并拥有弗兰克的母亲Miriam（Frosch）Jacobs的服装珠宝业务，他还帮助在林肯中开始了犹太寺庙。
 “有人说，作为一个宝宝，我在4/4的时间里咕噜咕噜，”雅各布斯先生在接受“弗兰克·雅各布：五十年的他最大的作品”（2015）采访时表示，这是一个被预测系列的第一本书称为“疯狂的最大作家。“ “在真理中，当我在学龄前我在学校时，我会回忆起押韵。” 
他毕业于内布拉斯加州大学林肯，他编辑了校园幽默杂志（并在一些经文中滑倒）。在军队雇用后，作为军事报纸和条纹的编辑和记者，他在一家公关公司工作，是新闻代理人的秘书，并为渔公鸡杂志写作。
 雅各斯先生在1957年4月发出的疯狂时，他的封面是埃及坟墓的墙上的封面般的吉祥物的疯狂形象。
 “正如我通过页面所融入的那样，我意识到我发现了我不知道我正在寻找的东西，”他被引用在2015年的工作中的纪念品。他在曼哈顿召集了杂志办公室，并与其出版商，威廉姆·纳夫斯，以及编辑，alfeldstein，并投放了他的想法。
 他们很快被拥抱。 1957年6月，问题包括雅各布先生撰写的若干特征，其中包括“棒球徒游”和“为什么我离开军队并成为一个平民。”
 这是一个长期结社的开始。他为杂志写了近600件;众多疯狂的平装书，就像运动，邮票和父亲这样的科目;和“William M. Geares的疯狂世界”（1972年），杂志的传记＆＃39;创始出版商的传记，他被描述为“最后的胖胖狂想”。
 他的最后一块发表于2014年，五年前疯狂停止经常出版的新材料，有利于使用新封面回收过去的材料。
  疯狂变得越来越少，雅各斯先生的产量减缓了越来越多的循环。 
在20世纪90年代中期，“新的流行文化取代了我非常熟悉的老流行文化，”他在2006年的疯狂商店采访时，是一个商品推销网站。 “我真的不是对新流行文化感兴趣的，但疯狂必须是它的读者。”  除了他的儿子，Jacobs先生在他的妻子，芭芭拉（Stellman）Jacobs幸存下来。  雅各布先生经常返回着名的诗歌的诗歌灵感。 他用Joyce Kilmer的“树木”来解体森林砍伐，并达图·乔治H.W. 布什在1988年的总统竞选期间，“凯西在蝙蝠”，以哀叹棒球的状态。 1991年，他将“共和国的战场赞美”转变为“被打击共和国的赞美诗”。 其中一个经文走了：  在我们城市的贫困和无家可归者住所的小巷里，你可以看到受害者从推动者出售的裂缝中死亡，而银行家从MedellínCartel那里洗钱 - 犯罪一直在进行游行！
note_en:
Frank Jacobs, an inventive satirist who in his 57 years at Mad magazine mocked popular culture and politics, often in pitch-perfect verse and lyrics, died on April 5 in Tarzana, Calif. He was 91.
  Mr. Jacobs brought a quick wit, a deep well of ideas and a love of rhyming to Mad in 1957, becoming one of that smart-alecky humor magazine’s most prolific contributors, especially during the 1960s and ’70s, when it was at the peak of its irreverence and its cultural influence.
 “He was the ultimate craftsman,” said John Ficarra, a former Mad editor. “He could be persnickety, for sure, but you had to respect him: He was in an endless search for the perfect word, the perfect phrase and the perfect rhyme.”
 Working with artists like  Mort Drucker (who died last year),  George Woodbridge and  Gerry Gersten, Mr. Jacobs parodied movie musicals like “Fiddler on the Roof” (which he turned into a sendup of suburbia in “Antenna on the Roof”); critiqued the policies of President Ronald Reagan in a line-by-line satire of Poe’s “The Raven”; wrote obituaries of comic-strip characters like the hapless office worker Dilbert (who suffocated from a lack of ventilation in his cubicle) and the working-class layabout Andy Capp (whose death was caused by a drunken driver); and devised Christmas carols for dysfunctional families.
  Arnie Kogen, a former Mad writer, said in an interview: “Frank’s stuff sparkled. It was smart, classy and always funny. He was the best writer Mad magazine ever had.”
 A fan of musical theater, Mr. Jacobs teamed with Mr. Drucker to turn “West Side Story” into “East Side Story,” a musical battle at the United Nations between gangs led by the United States and the Soviet Union. The Soviet premier (and gang leader)  Nikita Khrushchev sang:
 When you’re a Red  You’re a Red all the way  From your first Party purge  To your last power play!  When you’re a Red,  You’ve got agents galore;  You give prizes for peace  While they stir up a war.
 Mr. Jacobs’s parody of the  Great American Songbook prompted  Irving Berlin and a group of song publishers representing the work of Cole Porter,  Rodgers and Hammerstein and others to sue Mad’s parent company, E.C. Publications, for copyright infringement.
 At issue was “Sing Along With Mad,” a pullout section published in 1961 that consisted entirely of song parodies by Mr. Jacobs and Larry Siegel. Among them were  “Louella Schwartz Describes Her Malady” (a lampoon of Berlin’s “A Pretty Girl Is Like a Melody”) and “The First Time I Saw Maris” (a spoof of Jerome Kern and Oscar Hammerstein II’s “The Last Time I Saw Paris”), about the commercialization of the Yankee slugger  Roger Maris during the season he hit a record-breaking 61 home runs.
 He signed a contract with Gillette  To plug their razor blades.  And when he found he cut himself  He went and plugged Band-Aids!  The last time I saw Maris  He plugged six brands of beer!  The Democrats should pay him  To plug the New Frontier.
 In 1964, the United States Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit decided in Mad’s favor.
  In his opinion,  Judge Irving R. Kaufman ( most famous  for presiding over Julius and Ethel Rosenberg’s espionage trial) wrote, “The fact that defendants’ parodies were written in the same meter as plaintiffs’ compositions would seem inevitable if the original was to be recognized, but such a justification is not even necessary; we doubt that even so eminent a composer as plaintiff Irving Berlin should be permitted to claim a property interest in iambic pentameter.”
  Franklin Jacobs was born on May 30, 1929, in Lincoln, Neb. His father, David, was a traveling salesman and owned a costume jewelry business with Frank’s mother, Miriam (Frosch) Jacobs, who also helped start a Jewish temple in Lincoln.
 “Some say that as a baby I gurgled in 4/4 time,” Mr. Jacobs said in an interview for “Frank Jacobs: Five Decades of His Greatest Works” (2015), the first book in a projected series called “Mad’s Greatest Writers.” “In truth, I recall coming up with rhymes when I was in grade school.”
 He graduated from the University of Nebraska, Lincoln, where he edited the campus humor magazine (and slipped in some verse). After serving in the Army, as an editor and reporter for the military newspaper Stars and Stripes, he worked in a public relations firm, was the secretary to a press agent and wrote for Fishing Gazette magazine.
 Mr. Jacobs was bored and looking for direction when he bought the  April 1957 issue of Mad, whose cover image was of Alfred E. Neuman, the magazine’s gaptoothed mascot, on the wall of an Egyptian tomb.
 “As I leafed through the pages, I realized I’d found what I didn’t know I was looking for,” he was quoted as saying in the 2015 collection of his work. He called the magazine’s office in Manhattan and met with its publisher,  William M. Gaines, and editor,  Al Feldstein, and pitched his ideas.
 They were quickly embraced. The  June 1957 issue included several features written by Mr. Jacobs, among them “Baseball’s Hall of Shame” and “Why I Left the Army and Became a Civilian.”
 It was the beginning of a long association. He wrote nearly 600 pieces for the magazine; numerous Mad paperbacks, on subjects like sports, stamps and fathers; and “The Mad World of William M. Gaines” (1972), a biography of the magazine's founding publisher, whom he described as “the last of the great fat eccentrics.”
 His last piece was published in 2014, five years before  Mad ceased regular publication of new material in favor of recycling past material with new covers.
  As Mad became less relevant and its circulation tumbled, Mr. Jacobs’s output slowed.
 In the mid-1990s, “the new pop culture replaced the old pop culture that I was quite familiar with,”  he said in a 2006 interview for The Mad Store, a merchandising website. “I really wasn’t all that interested in the new pop culture, but Mad had to be, for its readership.”
 In addition to his son, Mr. Jacobs is survived by his wife, Barbara (Stellman) Jacobs.
 Mr. Jacobs often returned to well-known poems for parodic inspiration. He used Joyce Kilmer’s “Trees” to decry deforestation and to lampoon George H.W. Bush during his 1988 presidential campaign, and “Casey at the Bat” to lament the state of baseball. In 1991 he turned “The Battle Hymn of the Republic” into “The Hymn of the Battered Republic.” One of the verses went:
 In the alleys of our cities where the poor and homeless dwell,  You can see the victims dying from the crack that pushers sell,  While the bankers launder money from the Medellín cartel —  The crime keeps marching on!

自然条件影响户外运动。东京在7月下旬和8月初的＆＃34;有趣的＆＃34; - 它＆＃39;非常热，湿度额定导致约60％的时间。令人愉快的天气不会回到秋天，但电视要求推动奥运日程。一些耐力事件可能会在晚上运行，运动员将在下个月或两两个月增加他们的培训委员会的高温调理。过度的热量具有重要的物理和生理学后果，在过去十年中对生理学的理解发生了很大的变化（你可以在耐力事件中喝太多，电解质饮料无效）。也许是未来的帖子的东西，但现在让＆＃39; s坚持风。
    在奥运会期间，东京可能会在7英里/姆照片中有一致的风，主要来自南部或东方。要建立100和200米冲刺等的记录事件，110米的障碍和长跳跃和三重跳跃记录风速，并要求尾随风速小于每秒2米 - 约4.5英里/小时 - 在轨道上测量。风在比赛中很多东西，但如果风在合格事件之间变化很大，则可以偏见资格。
    你＆＃39觉得哈德风将始终是有利的障碍，但他们必须精神上调整他们的进步和各种风的发射点，只有最好的可以处理各种条件。长跳线必须调整他们的方法，尽可能接近起飞点而不是犯规。假设他们成功，尾随风可以是一个很大的优势。除了记录设置杆跳线类似。它们清除的高度与起飞速度的平方有关，所以尾风可以是一个很大的帮助。奇怪的是记录的风援助限制。也许即将到来的飓风在世界纪录的尝试中是有用的。
    封闭的道路课程种族是有问题的。人们可能会思考尾风和朝向部分平均出来，但如果风在恒定速度，则朝向部分总是较慢（这里＆＃39; s简单的解释）。当然存在鞋子性能的较大问题所以也许是我们＆＃39;重新记录的点，在跑步中的记录＆＃39; t。
    对于一些运动，风增加了一个有趣的维度。与其他大多数其他运动球相比，沙滩排球的密度低，所以风会产生很大的不同。另外，排球在空腹场地有一个有趣的空气动力学，每秒14到17米，速度常见于妇女繁荣，并被更好的球员利用。团队必须调整风速和方向，但事件经常在强大而疯狂的风中继续进行 - 有时候雨水，在奥运会期间在东京不可能。
    许多其他运动受到风的影响，但去年春天的最终项目在我教导的体育课程中的最终项目得到了我的注意。一个业余自行车赛车/生物学拟议的人力动力飞行。您将通过巧妙的踏板发电机收取电池。经过一定的时间，你＆＃39; D乘坐飞行加入你可以用腿部鼓起的任何力量来制作什么。寻找重量，充电时间和允许技术的正确限制会制造或打破这项运动。它可能会＆＃39; t是一个观众运动的tmuch，但它将为工程学生制作一个伟大的项目。
note_en:
Natural conditions impact outdoor sports.  Tokyo in late July and early August is " interesting" - it's very hot with humidity rated as oppressive about 60% of the time.  Pleasant weather doesn't return until the Fall, but TV demands drive the Olympic schedule.  Some endurance events will probably be run at night and athletes will be adding high temperature conditioning to their training routines in the next month or two.   Excessive heat has important physics and physiology consequences and the understanding of physiology has changed a lot in the past decade (you can drink too much in endurance events and electrolyte drinks are ineffective).  Perhaps something for future posts, but for now let's stick with the wind.
    During the Olympics Tokyo will probably have consistent winds around 7 mph mostly from the South or East.  To establish a record events like the 100 and 200 meter sprint, 110 meter hurdles and the long and triple jumps record the wind velocity and require a trailing windspeed to be less than 2 meters per second - about 4.5 mph - measured at the track.  The wind doesn't matter much within a race, but qualifications can be biased if the wind changes much between qualifying events.
    You'd think a tailwind would always be advantageous for hurdlers, but they have to mentally adjust their strides and launch points for a variety of winds and only the best can handle a variety of conditions.  Long jumpers have to adjust their approach to launch as close to the takeoff point as possible and not foul. Assuming they succeed, a trailing wind can be a big advantage.  Except for record setting pole vaulting is similar.  The height they clear is related to the square of takeoff speed so a tailwind can be a big help.  Oddly there's no wind assistance limit for records. Perhaps an approaching hurricane would be useful in world record attempts.
    Closed road course races are problematic.  One might think tailwind and headwind portions average out, but if the wind is at a constant velocity, the headwind portions are always slower ( here's a simple explanation).  Of course there's the larger problem of shoe performance so maybe we're at a point where records aren't terribly meaningful in running.
    For some sports the wind adds an interesting dimension.  The density of a beach volleyball is low compared to other most other sports balls, so the wind makes a big difference.  Additionally volleyballs have interesting aerodynamic properties in airspeeds between 14 and 17 meters per second - speeds commonly seen in the women's game and exploited by the better players.  Teams must to adjust for windspeed and direction, but events often go on in strong  and gusty winds - sometimes with heavy rain, which is not unlikely in Tokyo during the Olympics.
    Many other sports are impacted by the wind, but last Spring the final project in a science of sports class I taught got my attention.  An amateur bicycle racer/biology major proposed human powered flight.   You would charge a battery by frantically pedaling  generator.  After some amount of time you'd take flight adding whatever power you could muster with your legs to what the motor could produce. Finding the right limits for weight,  charging times, and allowable technologies would make or break the sport.  It probably wouldn't be tmuch of a spectator sport, but it would make a great project for engineering students.
title: 自然条件

    疫苗接种后行程的突然飙升与租赁汽车的相对短缺碰撞。租车公司卖出了一大群舰队 - 数十万车辆 - 在大流行中幸存下来。现在他们无法让汽车足够快，以满足新的需求，特别是通过半导体短缺停滞的汽车工厂。
  旅行者甚至在堪萨斯城，休斯顿和孟菲斯等非海滩目的地报告Sky-High价格和售罄的日期。即使是预订的旅行者也抱怨他们现在有时会出现，并且没有车，在没有车上，必须等待汽车返回并在驾驶之前恢复清洁。
  Parks Shackelford of华盛顿州D.C.，在3月下旬，在佛罗里达州杰克逊维尔，在西南航空公司上买了一张票。然后他发现他无法获得租车。他尝试了塔拉哈西 - 没运气。最后，他不得不取消他的不可盈亏飞行，并在佛罗里达边境附近的瓦尔托塔，帕尔多塔塔的三角洲航空公司票，因为他可以在那里开车。
note_en:
When you book a trip, do you start by shopping for convenient flights? Maybe reserving a favorite hotel?
    The sudden surge in  post-vaccination travel is colliding with a relative shortage of rental cars. Rental-car companies sold a huge chunk of their fleets—hundreds of thousands of vehicles—to survive the pandemic. Now they can’t get cars onto their lots fast enough to meet the new demand, especially with car factories stalled by  semiconductor shortages.
  Travelers report sky-high prices and sold-out dates even in non-beach destinations like Kansas City, Houston and Memphis. Even travelers with reservations complain that they now sometimes show up and, with no cars on the lot, must wait for a car to be returned and cleaned before they can drive off.
  Parks Shackelford of Washington, D.C., bought a ticket on Southwest Airlines to Jacksonville, Fla., for a hunting trip in southern Georgia in late March. Then he found he couldn’t get a rental car. He tried Tallahassee—no luck. Finally, he had to cancel his nonrefundable flight and buy a Delta Air Lines ticket to Valdosta, Ga., near the Florida border, because he could get a car there.
title: 为什么租车突然很难找到
title_en: Why rental cars are suddenly so hard to find

This software runs on your modern PC and acts as a bridge BBS interface. Connect your retro computers to it (via serial) and download software from the internet using a period approriate interface!
  This software is still in development, not everything is implemented, but it is useful as is.
 This software will help you transfer software to your classic Macintosh (e.g.MacOS System 6, 7, 68k Macs, etc) using the serial port. Very simply, thissoftware runs on a modern host computer, and behaves like a BBS would back inthe day. You connect your Macintosh to the host computer using serial, andthen, using your vintage computer, you can search and download files fromonline archives (e.g. Macintosh Garden, Mac Repository). However, this "BBS"has only one user, you!
  I've written this software to do what I want. It might not make sense to somepeople. I've lost interest in getting my _____ computer "connected" to theinternet. I like the BBS interface.  Ultimately I would like this software to be useful to Commodore, Apple ][,Tandy, etc users. But, I'm developing it mostly for my old macs, so it's notthere yet. If you would like to help connect it to online sources for thoseplatforms, please do!

要听到像纽约时报这样的发布商的音频故事，请下载iPhone或Android的奥迪。
 2018年，一个右右行动主义者Tommy Robinson发布了一个视频给YouTube声称，他已经被罗马的非洲移民袭击。
 缩略图图像和八字标题促进视频指示罗宾逊先生被火车站以外的黑人殴打。然后，在视频中，罗宾逊先生打了下巴的男人，把他扔到地上。
 该视频被视为超过280万次，它促使英国右翼小报的新闻故事促使新闻故事，罗宾逊先生对他的反移民和反伊斯兰观点来说迅速获得了绰号。
 对于Caolan Robertson  - 一个为罗宾逊先生工作的电影制作人，并帮助创建了视频 - 这是一个教学时刻。它显示了吸引对YouTube和其他社交媒体服务的关注所需的关键成分。
 该视频在英国和欧洲的反移民情绪中。它也集中在冲突上，在展示罗宾逊先生的拳头之前，在喊叫和帆船之间迅速切割。它也歪曲了实际发生的事情。
 在一个20秒的视频部分发布到Youtube，罗宾逊先生描述了他对罗马火车站的访问。 
“我们会选择最戏剧性的时刻 - 或假装它，使它看起来更加戏剧，”罗伯逊先生在最近的一次面试中表示。 “我们意识到，如果我们想要未来的YouTube，它必须由对抗驱动。每次我们做那种东西，它都会超越其他任何东西。“
 Robertson先生将继续为ALUREN SOUTHERN，STEFAN MOLYNEUX和ALEX JONES在内的大西洋双方的右翼YouTube人士的右翼Youtube人士提供视频。
 视频是为“回声室”量身定制的，这些“回声室”通常由YouTube等社交媒体网络创建。为了让你看，YouTube为您之前观看的人提供了类似的视频。但是有人看的时间越长，视频可以变得更加极端。
 “它可以创造像大师一样的那些非常激进的人，”这位前Youtube工程师Guillaume Chaslot表示，这是公司算法推动人们以极端内容的方式批评。 “在手表时间方面，大师很棒。”
 全球技术公司，监管机构和个人正在努力理解和控制YouTube和其他社交媒体服务的巨大力量。 2019年，公司发言人Farshad Shadloo在一份声明中表示，在2019年对我们推荐视频和仇恨内容的传播，使我们推荐误导和仇恨内容的重要变更。它禁止Molyneaux先生和琼斯先生。但极端的视频继续传播。
 及时，罗伯逊先生说，他意识到他在激烈的危险仇恨上工作的视频。在2019年，在英国的会议上由左翼报纸，罗伯特森先生向他的工作远离他的工作而偏离他的工作。他的心情变化了一些怀疑。
 “他被呈现为一个浪子儿子，”路易斯·加强主义者罗塞斯·莱斯·罗斯·罗伯斯先生的MEA CULPA。 “但他尚未持有账户。” 
现在，Robertson先生详细说明了他和他的合作者寻找对抗的方式，以获得YouTube的受欢迎程度。
 联系罗宾逊先生的努力取得了不成功，琼斯先生没有回复多个评论请求。南部女士表示，她不应该被描述为一个远方的活动家，说她只是一个保守派。她没有参与“一些可怕的远方筹集，试图欺骗人们观看我们的内容，”她补充道。 “我们只是在做任何其他Youtuber的所作所为。”
 罗马的剧集的原始镜头由罗伯逊先生提供并由纽约时报审查，表明YouTube视频被编辑，以给出罗宾逊先生受到威胁的虚假印象。全镜头显示他是侵略者。
 提供给时间的镜头的剪辑。罗马事件的原始镜头由罗伯逊先生提供的，展示了罗宾逊先生是侵略者。
 当男人注意到他正在拍摄街对面时，他走近了相机，罗宾逊先生将他推入迎面而来的车。随着该人抗议，称为罗宾逊先生疯狂，并告诉他以自己的生活，罗宾逊先生升级了这个论点。
 “这是一个方法会去，”他告诉那个男人。 “你将最终撞倒了无意识。”
 超过两年的时间，他帮助了罗伯森先生和其他人发布视频，罗伯逊先生了解如何使聪明的编辑和专注于对抗可以帮助吸引数百万视图的YouTube和其他服务。他还了解了YouTube的推荐算法如何常常向极端视频推动人们。 
“这意味着我们做了越来越极端的视频，”罗伯逊先生说。
 罗伯逊先生在爱尔兰长大，父母离婚后，他和父亲一起搬到了英格兰北部的主要工作室。从一个非常年轻的时候实现他是同性恋，他经常感觉像局外人一样。但他说，当他搬到伦敦的大学时，他遇到了更明显的同性恋恐惧症，并在城市东端的大部分穆斯林社区走路。
 在2016年在佛罗里达州奥兰多的同性恋夜总会拍摄之后。 - 其中穆斯林人对伊斯兰国家承诺忠诚造成49人并受伤53人 - 罗伯逊先生对穆斯林，特别是移民制定了极大的敌意。他说，他的愤怒被促进了大部分，他通过他观看YouTube的视频。
 他开始从主流网点看视频，就像HBO表演的一集“与比尔马赫的实时”，其中Sam Harris，作者和播客主持人，主张对穆斯林信仰的更大批评。 YouTube的推荐算法表明了更多极端的视频，涉及罗宾逊先生，如罗宾士和白人民族主义英国国家党的前成员，如斯蒂芬yaxley-lennon。
 2017年，Robertson先生联系了罗宾逊先生，很快就开始与他合作作为视频制作人。到年底，他也与加拿大的活动家南方南部女士合作。
 知道罗伯逊先生说，他和南方女士将设计出于产生冲突的人，罗伯逊先生说，他和南方的最关注。那12月，他们参加了伦敦的女士3月，南方女士参加了一部电视记者的一部分，接近了同样的四句话问题：“妇女权利或伊斯兰教？”
 根据Robertson先生的说法，他们经常收到困惑，衡量或礼貌的反应。他们继续问这个问题并提高了它。例如，南方女士表示，穆斯林妇女难以回答这个问题，因为他们的丈夫不会让他们参加3月。这导致愤怒在人群中建造。 
“它看来我们只是试图弄清楚发生了什么，收集信息，了解人们，”罗伯逊先生说。 “但是真的，我们正试图找到最燃烧的方式。”
  YouTube视频的缩略图图像表明了一个对抗：作为南方女士走开的女人尖叫着。正如他经常所做的那样，Robertson先生削尖了视频的视觉对比 - 淡化白色和黑色黑人 - 为了巧妙地让现场更加戏剧性。
 南部女士描述了不同的情况。 “我们问了这个问题，因为我们知道它会强迫人们质疑自己的政治观点，并意识到是一个坚定的女权主义者，也是支持宗教的矛盾，这是坦率地，坦率地区的妇女在妇女周围有质疑，”她说。并且，她补充说，他们使用了任何媒体公司将使用的视频技术。
 明年，Robertson先生和南方女士遍布南非，澳大利亚和新西兰创造了类似的视频。根据南部的YouTube频道女士的终身，根据当时审查的频道统计数据，她的视频被视为超过6300万次。
 超过71％的人查看这些视频尚未订阅她的YouTube频道。 2018年，在她受欢迎的高度，在YouTube的算法自动推荐给观众时至少发生了30％的观点。
 Molyneux先生避开了南部南方女士的冲突。他把自己作为一个在线哲学家。但是，罗伯特森先生的编辑在“令人满意的右翼观众上呼吁的最重要的想法”下滑，“他说。
 2018年，这对飞往波兰的一个视频，绘制了这个国家作为一个没有困难和冲突的地方。该子文本是因为它是因为波兰主要是白色的。在迄今为止的电子邮件中，Molyneux先生说：“在一个我不必雇用保护安全的国家是很好的。”他补充说，当他访问香港时，他觉得同样的方式。 
罗伯逊先生说，2019年初，他迷失了。 南部的YouTube频道女士上有一个明显的流量下降。 大约在同一时间，YouTube开始删除更多视频，公司思想鼓励暴力和传播错误信息。  在澳大利亚人在基督城的两名清真寺杀死了51人之后，新西兰在youtube传播的反移民信仰中推动 - 他说，他所造成的视频导致了同样的暴力 2016年奥兰多夜总会。  现在，Robertson先生监督划线电视，一个键入时间的视频过境。 他还经营着一个新的组织，自由的未来，旨在解除激进右翼极端分子。 他仍在计算YouTube的观点。  Robertson先生最近吹嘘在一天的文本中，一个视频目标是琼斯先生，他曾经与之合作的阴谋理论家，已被浏览超过25万次。

To hear more audio stories from publishers like The New York Times,   download Audm for iPhone or Android .
 In 2018, a far-right activist, Tommy Robinson, posted a video to YouTube claiming he had been attacked by an African migrant in Rome.
 The thumbnail image and eight-word title promoting the video indicated Mr. Robinson was assaulted by a Black man outside a train station. Then, in the video, Mr. Robinson punched the man in the jaw, dropping him to the ground.
 The video was viewed more than 2.8 million times, and it  prompted news stories across  the right-wing tabloids in Britain, where Mr. Robinson was rapidly gaining notoriety for his anti-immigrant and anti-Islamic views.
 For Caolan Robertson — a filmmaker who worked for Mr. Robinson and helped create the video — it was an instructional moment. It showed the key ingredients needed to attract attention on YouTube and other social media services.
 The video played into anti-immigrant sentiments in Britain and across Europe. It also focused squarely on conflict, cutting rapidly between shouts and shoves before showing Mr. Robinson’s punch. It also misrepresented what had actually happened.
 In a 20-second segment of the video posted to YouTube, Mr. Robinson describes his visit to a train station in Rome.
 “We would choose the most dramatic moment — or fake it and make it look more dramatic,” Mr. Robertson, 25, said in a recent interview. “We realized that if we wanted a future on YouTube, it had to be driven by confrontation. Every time we did that kind of thing, it would explode well beyond anything else.”
 Mr. Robertson would go on to produce videos for a who’s who of right-wing YouTube personalities on both sides of the Atlantic, including Lauren Southern, Stefan Molyneux and Alex Jones.
 The videos were tailored for the “echo chamber” that is often created by social media networks like YouTube. To keep you watching, YouTube serves up videos similar to those you have watched before. But the longer someone watches, the more extreme the videos can become.
 “It can create these very radical people who are like gurus,” said Guillaume Chaslot, a former YouTube engineer who has been critical of the way the company’s algorithms pushed people to extreme content. “In terms of watch time, a guru is wonderful.”
 Tech companies, regulators and individuals across the globe are struggling to understand and control the enormous power of YouTube and other social media services. In 2019, YouTube made “important changes to how we recommend videos and prevent the spread of misinformation and hateful content,” Farshad Shadloo, a spokesman for the company, said in a statement. It barred Mr. Molyneaux and Mr. Jones. But  extreme videos continue to spread.
 In time, Mr. Robertson said, he realized that the videos he worked on stoked dangerous hatred. And in 2019, at a conference in Britain run by a left-wing newspaper, The Byline Times, Mr. Robertson distanced himself from his work with the far right. His change of heart was met with some skepticism.
 “He was presented as a prodigal son,” said Louise Raw, an antifascist activist who was onstage for Mr. Robertson’s mea culpa. “But he has not been held to account.”
 Now, Mr. Robertson is detailing the ways he and his collaborators searched for confrontations to gain popularity on YouTube.
 Efforts to contact Mr. Robinson were unsuccessful, and Mr. Jones did not respond to multiple requests for comment. Ms. Southern said she should not be described as a far-right activist, saying she is merely a conservative. She was not involved in “some horrible far-right grift that tried to deceive people into watching our content,” she added. “We were just doing what any other YouTuber does.”
 Raw footage of the episode in Rome, provided by Mr. Robertson and reviewed by The New York Times, shows that the YouTube video was edited to give the false impression that Mr. Robinson was threatened. The full footage shows he was the aggressor.
 A clip from the footage provided to The Times. The raw footage of the incident in Rome, provided by Mr. Robertson, shows Mr. Robinson was the aggressor.
 When the man noticed he was being filmed from across the street, he approached the camera, and Mr. Robinson shoved him into an oncoming car. As the man protested, called Mr. Robinson crazy and told him to live his own life, Mr. Robinson escalated the argument.
 “There’s one way this is going to go,” he told the man. “You’re going to end up knocked down unconscious.”
 Over the more than two years he helped produce and publish videos for Mr. Robinson and others, Mr. Robertson learned how making clever edits and focusing on confrontation could help draw millions of views on YouTube and other services. He also learned how YouTube’s recommendation algorithm often nudged people toward extreme videos.
 “It meant that we did more and more extreme videos,” Mr. Robertson said.
 Mr. Robertson grew up in Ireland, and after his parents divorced, he moved with his father to a predominantly working-class area in the north of England. Realizing from a very young age that he is gay, he often felt like an outsider. But he said he encountered more overt homophobia when he moved to London for college and walked through the largely Muslim neighborhoods at the East End of the city.
 After the  2016 shooting at a gay nightclub in Orlando, Fla. — where a Muslim man pledging loyalty to the Islamic State killed 49 people and wounded 53 more — Mr. Robertson developed an extreme animosity toward Muslims, particularly immigrants. His anger was fueled in large part, he said, by videos he watched on YouTube.
 He began watching videos from mainstream outlets, like an episode of the HBO show “Real Time With Bill Maher” in which Sam Harris, an author and a podcast host,  advocated greater criticism of Muslim beliefs. YouTube’s recommendation algorithm suggested more-extreme videos involving personalities like Mr. Robinson, a former member of the neo-fascist and white nationalist British National Party who was born Stephen Yaxley-Lennon.
 In 2017, Mr. Robertson contacted Mr. Robinson and soon began working with him as a video producer. By the end of the year, he was also collaborating with Ms. Southern, an activist from Canada.
 Knowing what garnered the most attention on YouTube, Mr. Robertson said, he and Ms. Southern would devise public appearances meant to generate conflict. That December, they attended a women’s march in London and, with Ms. Southern playing the part of a television reporter, approached each woman with the same four-word question: “Women’s rights or Islam?”
 They often received a confused, measured or polite response, according to Mr. Robertson. They continued to ask the question and sharpened it. Ms. Southern, for example, said it would be difficult for Muslim women to answer the question because their husbands wouldn’t let them attend the march. That caused anger to build in the crowd.
 “It appears in the videos that we are just trying to figure out what is going on, gather information, understand people,” Mr. Robertson said. “But really, we were trying to find the most incendiary way of making them mad.”
  The thumbnail image for the YouTube video was indicative of a confrontation: a woman screaming as Ms. Southern walked away. As he often did, Mr. Robertson sharpened the video’s visual contrast — lightening the white colors and darkening the blacks — to subtly make the scene seem more dramatic.
 Ms. Southern described the situation differently. “We asked the question because we knew it was going to force people to question their own political views and realize the contradiction in being a hard-core feminist but also supporting a religion that, quite frankly, has questionable practices around women,” she said. And, she added, they used video techniques that any media company would use.
 The next year, Mr. Robertson and Ms. Southern traveled as far as South Africa, Australia and New Zealand to create similar videos. Over the lifetime of Ms. Southern’s YouTube channel, according to channel statistics reviewed by The Times, her videos were viewed over 63 million times.
 More than 71 percent of people who viewed these videos had not subscribed to her YouTube channel. In 2018, at the height of her popularity, at least 30 percent of the views occurred after the videos were automatically recommended to the viewer by YouTube’s algorithms.
 Mr. Molyneux shied away from the kind of conflict that Ms. Southern embraced. He fashioned himself as an online philosopher. But the material Mr. Robertson edited slipped in “far-right ideas that appealed to the ethnonationalists — the extreme right-wing audience,” he said.
 In 2018, the pair traveled to Poland for a video that painted the country as a place free of hardship and strife. The subtext was that it was because Poland is predominantly white. In an email to The Times, Mr. Molyneux said, “It was nice being in a country wherein I didn’t have to hire protective security.” He added that he felt the same way when he visited Hong Kong.
 By early 2019, Mr. Robertson said, he grew disillusioned. There was a noticeable drop in traffic on Ms. Southern’s YouTube channel. Around the same time, YouTube began to remove more videos the company thought encouraged violence and spread misinformation.
 After an Australian man killed 51 people at two mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand — driven in part by anti-immigrant beliefs propagated by YouTube — Mr. Robertson realized, he said, that the videos he had made led to the same kind of violence in the Orlando nightclub in 2016.
 Now, Mr. Robertson oversees Byline TV, a video offshoot of The Byline Times. He also runs a new organization, Future of Freedom, which seeks to de-radicalize right-wing extremists. He is still counting YouTube views.
 Mr. Robertson recently boasted in a text that in one day a video targeting Mr. Jones, the conspiracy theorist he once worked with, had been viewed over 250,000 times.

title_en: A Former Alt-Right YouTuber Explains His Methods
